game_name: Star Wars Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith

design_philosophy.core_loop: Linear progression through objective-based levels combining first-person shooting, lightsaber combat, and Force power usage against varied Star Wars enemies
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balance between blaster weapons (ranged combat) and lightsaber/Force powers (melee/magical), with emphasis shifted toward lightsaber and Force compared to Dark Forces II
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as trained Jedi Knights (Kyle Katarn and Mara Jade) undertaking missions for the New Republic while investigating Sith threats, culminating in a confrontation with a corrupted mentor
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Expansion enforces a single morally positive narrative path (unlike Dark Forces II's branching light/dark endings), prioritizing cohesive storytelling over player choice. Deliberately emphasizes Force powers and lightsaber combat over gunplay, signaling design intent to deepen Jedi fantasy over pure FPS mechanics

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Critics noted inconsistent difficulty across levels; no documented adaptive difficulty or accessibility options mentioned

progression.upgrade_system: Incremental unlocking of new weapons (blaster, thermal detonator, lightsaber) and Force powers; some powers damage-focused, others utility-focused
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-driven level progression with fourteen sequential levels, each with set completion objectives before advancing
replayability_hooks: Fifteen multiplayer maps with team-based and lightsaber-only variants
Character choice system with pre-set Star Wars characters (Luke, Vader, Boba Fett) each with distinct advantages/disadvantages
Capture-the-flag variant using ysalamir artifact with Force power suppression mechanics

world_building_techniques: Expands Star Wars Expanded Universe lore (set 10 ABY, 5 years post-Dark Forces II, during Dark Empire comic events)
Character-driven narrative featuring protagonist transition from Kyle Katarn to Mara Jade with established character arcs
Environmental storytelling through mission locations (Altyr V New Republic outpost, Dromund Kaas Sith temple)
NPC interactions with both hostile and non-hostile characters providing context and assistance
Climactic confrontation revealing mentor corruption by dark side, creating emotional narrative payload

boss_design.philosophy: Story-integrated final confrontation rather than traditional boss encounters; climax involves duel with corrupted mentor (Kyle Katarn) as narrative culmination of Mara Jade's journey
notable_bosses: Kyle Katarn (final duel) - corrupted by Sith temple dark side influence

faction_indicators: New Republic (light side affiliation) - player faction providing missions
Sith (dark side) - corrupting influence manifested through Dromund Kaas temple
danger_signals: Sith temple investigation triggers mentor corruption
Dark side power explicitly shown as corrupting force affecting allies
Imperial weapons platforms disguised as asteroids - deception threat
environmental_storytelling: Dromund Kaas Sith temple as source of corruption
New Republic outpost under Imperial attack - faction conflict visualization
Temple architecture implying ancient Sith presence and lingering dark side influence

sources_used: wikipedia - Star Wars Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith
